content:
When Your Architecture Starts Fighting Back

8 min read
2 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Circular dependencies rarely begin with one obviously bad decision. They grow from convenient imports, shared services, feature-to-feature shortcuts, and unclear ownership. Learn how enterprise Angular teams prevent dependency cycles before they turn a maintainable application into a tightly coupled system.
Your Application Still Works
That’s what makes circular dependencies dangerous.
The application compiles.
The page loads.
The feature works.
Nothing immediately tells you that your architecture is slowly becoming harder to change.
It usually begins with one innocent import.
The Orders feature needs customer information.
import { CustomerService } from
'../../customers/services/customer.service';Later, the Customer feature needs order history.
import { OrderService } from
'../../orders/services/order.service';Now the relationship looks like this:
Customers
↓
Orders
↓
Customers
